pattern: Counting subarrays with exactly k distinct values equals the count with at most k minus the count with at most k-1, each computed by a sliding window that grows the answer by its width.
hints: Counting exactly k distinct directly is awkward, but counting at most k distinct is a clean sliding window.
Exactly k equals (at most k) minus (at most k-1).
Write a helper atMost(m): expand the right edge, shrink from the left while more than m distinct remain, and add (right - left + 1) to the count each step; return atMost(k) - atMost(k-1).

edge_inputs.description: k larger than total distinct yields zero
all identical with k one counts every subarray
all distinct with k equal to length

stress.description: moderate array over a small value range with random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    vmax = max(1, n // 3)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, vmax) for _ in range(n)]
    distinct = len(set(nums))
    k = rng.randint(1, max(1, distinct))
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: k bounded by distinct count so the answer is frequently non-trivial
